name: Crew Arrival at Kennedy
description: The Artemis II crew arrives at Kennedy Space Center and answers questions from credentialed media.

name: Prelaunch News Conference
description: NASA holds a prelaunch news conference on countdown status with launch team representatives and weather officers.

name: Tanking Operations Begin
description: Loading of liquid hydrogen into the SLS core stage begins with a slow fill to chill down the tank and feed lines.

name: Artemis II Liftoff
description: SLS lifts off from LC-39B at Kennedy Space Center. Nearly 40 MN of thrust pushes the crew toward orbit at ~8 km/s.

name: Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) Burn
description: THE critical burn — ESM main engine fires for ~6 min 5 s, adding ~1,450 km/h to escape Earth orbit and set course for the Moon.

name: Live Crew Downlink — Day 3
description: Live video communication with the crew as they coast toward the Moon. First public view of the crew in deep space.

name: Outbound Trajectory Correction Burn 3
description: Outbound trajectory correction burn 3 — 17.5 seconds. Refines Orion's path to the Moon.

name: Enter Lunar Sphere of Influence
description: Orion enters the lunar sphere of influence — the Moon's gravity becomes the dominant force controlling the spacecraft's trajectory.

name: Apollo 13 Distance Record Broken
description: The crew surpasses the record for humans' farthest distance from Earth, previously set by Apollo 13 in 1970 at 248,655 miles.

name: Loss of Communications (Blackout)
description: Predicted loss of communications as crew heads behind the Moon. Approximately 40 minutes of blackout.

name: Lunar Closest Approach — 6545 km Above Surface
description: Orion makes its closest approach to the Moon at 6545 km (4067 statute miles) above the surface.

name: Maximum Distance from Earth — 406,770 km
description: Orion reaches maximum distance from Earth: 406,770 km (252,756 statute miles) — the farthest any humans have ever traveled.

name: Solar Eclipse from Lunar Orbit
description: Solar eclipse from lunar orbit — the Sun passes behind the Moon from the crew's perspective. Lasts approximately 57 minutes.

name: Crew Speaks with ISS Astronauts
description: Audio conversation between the Artemis II crew and astronauts aboard the International Space Station.

name: Return Trajectory Correction Burn 1
description: First of three return course-correction burns to adjust Orion's path toward the re-entry corridor.

name: Crew News Conference from Space
description: The Artemis II crew holds a press conference from deep space, answering questions from journalists on Earth.

name: Service Module Separation
description: The European Service Module separates from the crew module at about 7:33 p.m. EDT, exposing Orion's heat shield for entry.

name: Splashdown in Pacific Ocean
description: Orion splashes down near San Diego at about 8:07 p.m. EDT. U.S. Navy recovery teams retrieve the crew and capsule.
